commit.message:
Fix session persistence and enhance UI design

Session fix:
- Auth callback now sets cookies directly on redirect response object
  instead of relying on cookies() API which doesn't carry over to redirects
- Middleware now copies auth cookies to all redirect responses via
  redirectWithCookies helper, preventing session loss on navigation

Design enhancements (Remotion-inspired):
- Sidebar nav items with 3D border/shadow effects on hover and active state
- Dashboard cards with elevation shadows and hover transitions
- Upload card as primary CTA with arrow animation
- Search bar with bordered input style
- Bolder typography throughout (font-black, tighter tracking)
- Header with backdrop blur effect
- User footer with bordered card style

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: set recap_email_sent=True even when 0 emails sent

Prevents infinite retry loop when SMTP is misconfigured. The flag now
marks the attempt as complete regardless of delivery count, with a
warning log when 0 emails were sent so admins can investigate.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
UI/UX overhaul: commercial-ready design system

- New design system with brand tokens (navy/gold/off-white), Google Fonts
- Premium sidebar: navy bg, gold accents, notification banner, badges
- Dashboard: greeting, stat cards, quick actions, deadlines, activity feed
- Cases: search, filter pills, data table, summary bar, row actions
- Settings: 5-tab layout (Profile, Firm, Notifications, Security, Billing)
- Reusable CSS classes for cards, buttons, badges, tables, animations

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Render deploy button to README

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: HTML email templates + results recap notification

Upgrade all emails from plain text to polished HTML matching the
Greenside Ledger design system (Augusta-inspired greens, gold accents,
Georgia serif headings). All templates are Gmail-safe: inline styles
only, table-based layout, system font stacks, no <style> blocks.

Changes:
- send_email() now sends MIMEMultipart('alternative') with both
  plain text and HTML parts for graceful fallback
- Picks Open email: tournament card with purse/deadline, green CTA button
- Deadline reminders: urgency-escalating visual treatment across
  24h (green), 12h (amber), 1h (red) windows
- Results Recap (net new): personalized per-user email with their
  pick result, season rank, and top-3 weekly leaderboard table
- recap_email_sent flag on Tournament prevents double-sends
- _app_context() helper avoids nested session issues when called
  from sync_api.py CLI commands vs standalone scheduled tasks
- Trigger wired in sync_api.py after process_tournament_picks()
  in all finalization paths (sync-earnings, sync-run results/earnings)

commit.message:
feat: enforce tier-based feature restrictions across backend and frontend

Add require_tier() dependency (backend/api/dependencies.py) and TierGate
component (frontend/components/ui/tier-gate.tsx) for subscription-based
access control.

Backend: 133 tier checks across 13 route files — Starter+ (social,
wordpress, projects, analytics/GSC, keyword research), Professional+
(competitor, reports, knowledge, bulk, tags, templates, decay/AEO/revenue),
Enterprise (agency). Returns 403 with upgrade message.

Frontend: 38 dashboard pages wrapped with TierGate showing lock icon
and upgrade prompt when user's tier is insufficient.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: разрешить показ текущего экрана заново (same-screen refresh)

show_screen() блокировала переход MAIN_MENU -> MAIN_MENU через can_navigate,
из-за чего /start не отображал главное меню если пользователь уже был на нём.
Добавлена проверка current_screen != screen_id, аналогично _handle_navigation_action.

h3 framework caused ERR_REQUIRE_ESM and export validation errors.

which is exactly what @vercel/node expects as a serverless handler.

commit.message:
Midday dashboard rebuild 2026-03-09

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: revert long-polling change that broke Brave Shields compatibility

experimentalForceLongPolling rerouted ALL Firestore traffic (including
one-time getDoc REST calls) through a blocked transport in Brave, causing
profile loading to fail and lessons to never generate.

The app only uses one-time reads (getDoc/getDocs) — no real-time listeners.
The default getFirestore() uses the REST API directly, which works fine in
Brave with Shields enabled. The WebChannel background noise in the console
is harmless when no onSnapshot listeners are registered.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Add missing OAuth providers (HubSpot, Shopify, Instagram, Threads)
- Add once trigger type, compaction SSE event, 502 status code
- Fix billing plan/checkout/portal response shapes to match actual API
- Fix channel response shape (object keyed by name, not array)
- Fix session response shape (timestamps as numbers, added fields)
- Add JavaScript and Python code examples to all endpoint pages
- Add WSL to install label
- Update legal pages

Fix snap-to-ground accumulation during engine-controlled movement

- Suppress snap-to-ground when prevRequestedVelocity.y >= 0 (strict check)
  to avoid snapping during movePlayerTo interpolation where requested
  velocity is zero
- Reset prevRequestedVelocity alongside velocity when an external override
  is detected, so snap is also suppressed on that same tick
- Remove debug movePlayerTo test system

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add defensive array checks on all API response handling

All client pages now verify API responses are arrays before calling
.find(), .filter(), .map() etc. Prevents "v.find is not a function"
crashes when an API returns an error object instead of an array.

Affected pages: dashboard, cases list, PRW, skills, profiles,
analysis, and results.

commit.message:
Add null guard to SurvivorAnims AnimSetInt to prevent GetHealth01 crash

When Expansion AI proxy entities flood the engine with GetHealth01 errors
on the client, engine state can become corrupted. The next AnimSetInt call
on the local player's ModCommandHandlerAfter then crashes with an access
violation at a corrupted heap address. Adding a null check on
GetAnimInterface() prevents the crash.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix critical audit findings: RNG fairness, motherlode access, polling, shop

- C3: Fix operator RNG manipulation — add DEPLOY_PHASE_BLOCKS constant,
  targetBlock set at startRound(), closeDeploys() uses fixed block hash
  instead of letting operator shop for favorable blocks
- C4: withdrawMotherlode now onlyOwner + nonReentrant, sends to teamWallet
  instead of arbitrary operator-chosen address
- C5: Add mutex to createPoller preventing overlapping poll ticks
- C6: Stop game service in ServiceProvider init cleanup to prevent zombies
- C7: Shop deductBalance now burns DRILL on-chain via transfer to 0xdead
  instead of being a no-op in contract mode
- Update getRoundState to return targetBlock, update all ABIs + operator
- Update all Solidity tests for new deploy phase block advancement

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: correct MariaDB download URL for macOS x64

macOS X64 was incorrectly pointing to ARM64 binaries.
Changed from bintar-macos-arm64 to bintar-macos-x86_64.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add thank-you page and form redirect for booking form

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix VUI: correct detectable lift threshold to ~3.9pp per cohort

~600 control clients per cohort means ~3.9pp needed for significance,
not 1.29pp (which was the pooled/overall figure). Even best cohort
(Feb 2026 +3.61pp, p=0.073) falls just short. Increasing control to
15% (~4,200/cohort) brings threshold to ~1.5pp.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: 添加 CLAUDE.md 开发规范

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: enable BFF→API communication in dev via DEV_BYPASS_RBAC

The web BFF was getting 401s from RBAC-protected routes because
RequireRole requires X-User-ID + DB membership, which the BFF
cannot provide without JWT/Cognito integration.

- Add DEV_BYPASS_RBAC=true bypass in RequireRole (injects RoleOwner,
  skips all identity/membership checks when env var is set)
- Set DEV_BYPASS_RBAC=true in compose.override.yaml and .example
- Read API_AUTH_TOKEN from env in web NewAPIClient (falls back to
  "dev-token" when unset)
- Add TestRequireRole/DEV_BYPASS_RBAC test case using t.Setenv
- Add TestNewAPIClient_AuthHeader tests for the token env var logic

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix foto pipeline: 6 breekpunten opgelost van scanner tot products.json

A. github-sync dedup key: platform::name::condition (was platform::name)
   Voorkomt dat dual listings (Goed + Redelijke staat) elkaar overschrijven

B. Image URLs zonder timestamps in products.json
   Strip ?t=xxx bij opslag in add-product + github-sync normalisatie
   Voorkomt stale cached URLs die nooit updaten

C. imageCount afleiden uit werkelijke imageUrls keys
   Niet meer vertrouwen op client-input voor image count

D. v2 cache invalidatie retry bij upload-image
   Als eerste delete faalt, retry 1x — oude cached foto is erger dan 200ms

E. Image existence check bij github-sync
   Valideer dat image blob keys bestaan, null-out ontbrekende images
   Producten zonder werkende foto krijgen null zodat shop geen 404 toont

F. product-image Sharp fallback
   Als Sharp optimalisatie crasht, serveer origineel (beter dan 404)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Buttons now use pill shape (rounded-full) with clean hover states

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: 7z VFS provider ignores configured exe path, fails on Windows

SevenZipVfsProvider.Initialize() only tried PATH candidates (7z/7za/7zz),
never the user-configured path from Options → Configuration.
On Windows, 7-Zip lives in C:\Program Files\7-Zip\ which is not on PATH,
so _exe stayed null and CanHandle returned false → "No VFS provider found".

- Add constructor parameter configuredPath to SevenZipVfsProvider
- Initialize() tries configuredPath first, then PATH, then the default
  Windows install location as a last resort
- Pass settings.SevenZipPath from AppSetup
- Improve EnsureInstalled() error message to mention the settings field

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Warden: provider-specific verdict parsing for Claude/Copilot/Gemini (Forge-wrj) (#154)

* feat(warden): provider-specific verdict parsing for Claude/Copilot/Gemini

Add provider-aware fallback heuristics to parseVerdict so the Warden can
reliably extract verdicts regardless of which AI backend produced the
review. Each provider now has a tailored parsing strategy:

- Claude: JSON-first with minimal string fallback (existing behavior)
- Gemini: JSON fragments, key-value lines (Verdict: approve), prose signals
- Copilot (Haiku): aggressive natural language parsing for approval,
  rejection, and request-changes phrases

Phase 1 (structured JSON extraction) remains shared across all providers.
Phase 2 dispatches to provider-specific parsers only when JSON fails.

Also adds extractKeyValueVerdict() for "Verdict: approve" style lines
and containsAny() helper for prose signal matching.

Bead: Forge-wrj

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review comments for Forge-wrj

- extractKeyValueVerdict: add "verdict :" and "**verdict** :" patterns to
  match optional whitespace before colon (fixes comment 1)
- parseGeminiVerdict: guard "changes need" heuristic against "no changes needed"
  approval phrase; tighten to "changes needed"/"changes need to" (fixes comment 2)
- parseCopilotVerdict: same guard applied to copilot fallback parser (fixes comment 3)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add button-abort to blocking wait loops via emergency_stop()

Pressing the user button while a profile is active now causes the robot
to stop immediately rather than continuing to run indefinitely.

- motion.h: add emergency_stop() which calls reset_drive_system() then
  disable_drive(). This is the shared safe-state entry point for the
  motion layer.
- motion.h: spin_turn() forward-bleed loop and wait_until_position()
  both check switches.button_pressed() and call emergency_stop() on press.
- profile.h: wait_until_finished() checks switches.button_pressed() and
  calls this->stop() (target speed → 0, state → PS_FINISHED), causing
  the systick PID to ramp motors down. Returns immediately so the caller
  can handle the abort.
- mouse.h: panic() now calls motion.emergency_stop() instead of the two
  separate reset_drive_system()/disable_drive() calls. Sensor shutdown
  and operator interaction (LED blink, button wait) remain in panic().

Closes #25 (FR-10)

commit.message:
Bug: PRs still appear in Ready to Merge while Copilot review is pending (Forge-6sc) (#152)

* fix: prevent PRs from appearing in Ready to Merge while Copilot review is pending

The race condition where new PRs briefly appeared in Ready to Merge before
GitHub assigned Copilot as a reviewer has regressed. The root cause is that
InsertPR defaulted has_pending_reviews to 0 (no pending reviews), and the
immediate CheckStatusLight call after PR creation ran before GitHub had
assigned reviewers asynchronously.

Three-part fix:
1. InsertPR now explicitly sets has_pending_reviews=1, so new PRs assume
   reviews are pending until bellows confirms otherwise ("guilty until
   proven innocent").
2. CheckStatusLight in ghpr.Create() no longer clears has_pending_reviews
   to 0 — it preserves the safe default and lets bellows be the
   authoritative source for clearing the flag.
3. The daemon merge handler's live readiness check now also verifies
   HasPendingReviewRequests(), adding defense in depth.

Bead: Forge-6sc

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review comments for Forge-6sc

- Update comment to accurately describe that CheckStatusLight only checks
  merge conflict state, not unresolved threads; clarify Bellows is the
  authoritative source for threads and has_pending_reviews
- Inline the two fields actually used from CheckStatusLight result instead
  of calling MergeabilityFromStatus (which includes HasPendingReviews),
  making it explicit that pending reviews are intentionally kept at the
  safe default (true) regardless of the light check result

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix black over-bearing validation — was checking wrong direction

The over-bearing check for black looked at points 23-24 (closer to
bear-off) instead of 19 to from-1 (further from bear-off). This caused
legal over-bearing moves to be rejected during bear-off, making the AI
fall back to random moves or fail mid-sequence.

Also remove debug logging and handle object response format from GNUBG.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

  - sera_ai/api/app.py: before_request hook, /api/sistem/saglik muaf
  - Key tanımlı değilse dev modu (uyarıyla açık)
  - 15 yeni test: 401/404/201 senaryoları dahil

config.yaml tamamlandı:
  - BildirimKonfig: kanal aktiflik bayrakları + env var adları
  - SistemKonfig: api_key_env, bildirim alanları eklendi
  - settings.py: tüm yeni alanlar parse ediliyor

Testler: 63 passed (48 eski + 15 yeni API auth testi)
